question_text: what is the max temperature that 6000-2RSH/GFJ can tolerate?
answer: 100 °C. Temperatures up to 120 °C (250 °F) can only be tolerated for brief periods.

question_text: what is the minimum temperature that 6000-2RSH/GFJ can tolerate?
answer: Damage can result when the bearings are operated continuously below 10 °C. At the temperature of -40 °C, the torque required to start or run this bearing may be significantly higher. Below -40 °C is also not allowed for the seals used in this bearing.

question_text: what is the max temperature that 6206-2RS2/C3GJN can tolerate?
what is the minimum temperature that 6206-2RS2/C3GJN can tolerate?
answer: Damage can result when the bearings are operated continuously below 35 °C. At the temperature of -30 °C, the torque required to start or run this bearing may be significantly higher. Below -30 °C is also not allowed for the seals used in this bearing.

question_text: what is the max temperature that 6206-2RS2/C3S1GWP can tolerate?
answer: Temperatures above the 145 °C can be tolerated for short periods but may have an impact on the grease life. At 167 °C the grease used in this bearing will lose its structure and becomes a fluid.

question_text: what is the minimum temperature that 6206-2RS2/C3S1GWP can tolerate?
answer: Damage can result when the bearings are operated continuously below -22 °C. Below -30 °C is not allowed for the seals used in this bearing.

question_text: what is the max temperature that QJ 206 MA can tolerate?

I have a 6000-2RSH/GFJ which is said to tolerate temperature up to 100 degree C. I want to change the lubricant to tolerate higher temperature, saying around 140 degree C. Which lubricant should I use?
answer: Changing lubricant would not allow this bearing to tolerate higher temperature because the limiting factor in this case is the seals used.

question_text: What are the temperature limits for Viton rubber seals? (Viton is a trademark, often used as a product name)
answer: The FKM rubber seals have a temperature limit up to 200°C

question_text: What is the maximum temperature for sensor bearings?
answer: It is 125°C during 500 hrs

question_text: Can TLSD 250 be used in -20°C?
answer: Lowest temperature is 0°C

question_text: What is the viscosity of the dismounting oil?
answer: This oil LHDF 900 has a viscosity of 900 mm2/s at 20°C

question_text: what will happen with my spherical plain bearing when operating in 180°C?
answer: The load carrying capacity is reduced when temperature is above 120°C

question_text: How long time can I store my open bearings?
answer: 10 years if the humidity is below 65% and temperature is btw 20-25°C

question_text: What is the most common grease to use for my spherical plain bearings?
answer: If steel/steel combination the most common grease is LGHB 2, LGEP 2 or LGMT 3

question_text: What is the most common grease to use for my YAR 205-2F bearings?
answer: LGWA 2 or LGMT 2
